mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate signals tightening labor markets that boost consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE falls below 5.5%, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and real GDP indicates broad-based economic expansion that lifts corporate profit margins, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in more than 55% of out-of-sample periods, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression yields a p-value above 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production peaking and subsequently declining signals capacity constraints and weakening aggregate demand, which compresses equity risk premiums and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls for two consecutive months
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of triggered episodes, or the signal's information coefficient drops below zero when adjusted for FEDFUNDS trajectory.

mechanism_narrative: Rising residential investment measured by housing starts provides a leading indicator for broader economic activity, mechanically translating into positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day trading window.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in more than 50% of observations, or the rolling 1-year correlation between HOUST changes and SPX 5-day returns falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces aggressive monetary tightening, which increases discount rates and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally in more than 60% of high-inflation episodes, or the forward return differential between high and low inflation regimes is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in nonfarm payrolls and industrial production confirms a self-reinforcing economic cycle that sustains corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields a negative Sharpe ratio over any 3-year rolling window, or the joint probability of positive returns does not exceed the baseline market return probability by at least 10 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in residential construction activity signals improving household balance sheets and wealth effects, which mechanically supports consumer discretionary spending and lifts S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS rate changes.
